steps.title: Use the given function value
steps.content:
Substitute $x=c$ into the function. The squared term becomes $0$, so the given value of $p(c)$ can be used to solve for the constant:
$$p(c)=\frac{(c-c)^2+180}{2c}=\frac{180}{2c}=\frac{90}{c}$$
Since $p(c)=10$:
$$\frac{90}{c}=10$$
$$c=9$$

steps.title: Evaluate the function
steps.content:
Now substitute $x=15$ and $c=9$ into the original function, then simplify the numerator and denominator completely:
$$p(15)=\frac{(15-9)^2+180}{2(9)}$$
$$p(15)=\frac{36+180}{18}=\frac{216}{18}=12$$
The answer is D.
